source: seed (fallback) — representative examples from public sources

items.title: Clashes on the eastern front: operations continue in the Donbas
items.description: Ukrainian and Russian forces are contesting key positions along the front line, while the EU debates a new military aid package.

items.title: Oil prices climb: the Red Sea back in the spotlight
items.description: Fresh Houthi attacks push Brent and WTI higher. Shipping operators reroute vessels around the Cape of Good Hope.

items.title: Gaza: new humanitarian operations, UN calls for safe access
items.description: United Nations agencies warn of food and medicine shortages. Truce talks are ongoing in Cairo.

items.title: Sudan: RSF and SAF still far from a deal, 11 million displaced
items.description: Sudan's humanitarian crisis is the world's largest by number of internally displaced people, according to IOM.

items.title: Wheat and corn: prices under pressure from geopolitical tensions
items.description: CBOT posts a rebound after weeks of weakness. Concerns over Black Sea exports persist.

items.title: Myanmar: junta loses ground in some north-eastern regions
items.description: Coordinated resistance forces seize outposts along the border with China.

items.title: Haiti: the multinational mission redefines priorities in Port-au-Prince
items.description: The Kenyan contingent requests more resources as gangs control broad areas of the capital.

items.title: European gas: contained volatility, stocks above seasonal average
items.description: TTF steady in mid-April. Operators monitor LNG supplies and summer maintenance.

items.title: Kivu: UN reports rising violence against civilians
items.description: More than 400,000 newly displaced in eastern DRC over the past few months.

items.title: Sahel: new jihadist offensive hits villages in northern Burkina
items.description: Dozens of casualties. Authorities call for stronger regional military presence.

items.title: Shipping: freight rates remain elevated versus pre-crisis levels
items.description: Drewry reports the SCFI index slightly lower but still 40% above the 2019 average.

items.title: Russia–Ukraine: pressure on the Kharkiv front, new NATO supplies
items.description: Drone strikes intensify on both fronts, while new air-defence systems arrive for allies.

items.title: Consumer electronics: prices stable despite chip tensions
items.description: Manufacturers optimise inventories; Taiwan risks remain the main source of uncertainty.

items.title: Lebanon: exchanges of fire on the southern border, some families return
items.description: Foreign ministries urge the utmost caution for travellers.

items.title: Afghanistan: child malnutrition, UNICEF calls for additional funding
items.description: More than 3 million children at risk of severe acute malnutrition in 2026.

items.title: Emerging markets: Indian rupee stable, South African rand under pressure
items.description: Investors monitor capital flows amid a complicated geopolitical backdrop.
